task: Xu Youxin paper Type-3 DFIG low-voltage LVRT trip criterion audit

read_methods: PyMuPDF page rendering for visual check

figure.title: 风电机组故障电压曲线
figure.source_type: rendered PDF page and extracted text

low_voltage_points_observed.evidence: figure lower LVRT boundary and nearby text
figure rising LVRT boundary and nearby text

direct_text_facts: The section states that wind turbines should stay grid-connected for 625 ms when the POC voltage drops to 20% of rated voltage.
The section states that the voltage should recover to 90% of rated voltage within 2 s after voltage sag begins.
The section introduces a wind turbine trip event and sets the voltage ride-through protection delay tVRT according to POC voltage Vs.
formula_facts: Equation (2-8) gives tVRT = 0 when Vs <= 0.20 or Vs >= 1.30.
Equation (2-8) gives a linear low-voltage branch for 0.20 < Vs < 0.90.
Equation (2-8) includes high-voltage branches for 1.10 to 1.30 pu, outside this low-voltage-only implementation scope.
figure_facts: Figure 2-3 shows a low-voltage ride-through boundary at about 0.20 pu until t0+0.625 s.
Figure 2-3 shows a rising recovery boundary from about 0.20 pu at 0.625 s to about 0.90 pu at 2.0 s.
evidence_text_summary.conflict: The text/figure support 0.625 s at 0.20 pu, while equation (2-8) assigns tVRT = 0 to Vs <= 0.20.

piecewise_rule.reason_not_final: The boundary Vs = 0.20 pu is inconsistent between text/figure and equation (2-8), so the complete low-voltage implementation rule requires manual confirmation.

candidate_immediate_trip.evidence: Equation (2-8) first branch; exact equality remains unresolved.

candidate_exit_low_voltage_timer.action: do not continue low-voltage trip timing
candidate_exit_low_voltage_timer.evidence: The low-voltage branch is open at 0.90 and text describes recovery to 90% within 2 s.

Vs_0_20.equation_candidate: tVRT = 0 s because equation uses Vs <= 0.20 in the zero-delay branch
Vs_0_20.recommended_before_implementation: Ask user to confirm which boundary convention to use before PSCAD external LVRT logic is implemented.

project_implementation_decision.decision: Vs <= 0.20 pu maps to tVRT = 0 s and immediate_trip
project_implementation_decision.decision_source: Explicit user-confirmed project implementation convention for follow-on prototype work
project_implementation_decision.evidence_limit: This records a project boundary choice and does not erase the paper text/figure versus equation boundary tension at exactly Vs = 0.20 pu.

Vs_0_90.evidence: Equation low-voltage linear branch is 0.20 < Vs < 0.90; text states recovery to 90% within 2 s.
Vs_0_90.candidate_action: exit or reset low-voltage trip timing at and above 0.90 pu, unless later manual confirmation changes this convention.

trip_timer_interpretation.paper_basis: tVRT is introduced as the delay action time of wind turbine voltage ride-through protection according to POC voltage Vs.
trip_timer_interpretation.engineering_translation: When the POC voltage remains in the low-voltage region, compare the low-voltage duration with tVRT(Vs); trip when the duration exceeds the allowed time.
limitations: The nearby paper text does not specify whether the timer should use instantaneous Vs, latched minimum Vs, or a different implementation detail.
The exact equality convention at Vs = 0.20 pu must be manually confirmed before implementation.

candidate_pscad_input.role: future candidate input for external LVRT trip prototype
basis: Existing project documentation maps VIBR1_2 to the Type-3 branch 22 kV POC/collector-side voltage output.
Fault resistance sweep results already use VIBR1_2 as the voltage sag depth comparison signal.
limitations: VIBR1_2 base and sampling semantics should be confirmed before using it as paper Vs.
No PSCAD LVRT module or breaker trip logic has been implemented in this task.

scope_alignment: The audit is aligned with the paper's Section 2.3.2 low-voltage ride-through trip delay criterion.
The confirmed interior low-voltage branch can support later external LVRT prototype design after boundary confirmation.
scope_gaps: The current PSCAD project is a single Type-3 DFIG replacement prototype, not the paper's complete multi-wind-farm cascading-failure model.
High-voltage branches in equation (2-8), frequency protection, overload protection, UFLS/UVLS, line tripping, MATLAB coupling, and cascading logic are outside this task.
The exact implementation convention at Vs = 0.20 pu is unresolved.
confirmed_facts: PDF page 20 contains Figure 2-3 and equation (2-8).
The low-voltage interior branch of equation (2-8) is 0.20 < Vs < 0.90.
The low-voltage interior branch connects 0.625 s at 0.20 pu to 2.0 s at 0.90 pu.
Equation (2-8) assigns zero delay for Vs <= 0.20, creating a boundary conflict with the text/figure at exactly 0.20 pu.

ready_parts: Use the linear formula for 0.20 < Vs < 0.90.
For this project implementation path, treat Vs <= 0.20 as tVRT = 0 s and immediate_trip by explicit user boundary decision.
Treat Vs at or above 0.90 and below high-voltage branches as a recovery/no-low-voltage-timer candidate.
not_ready_parts: Do not implement exact Vs = 0.20 behavior until manually confirmed.
Do not claim this is a complete paper model reproduction.

unresolved_items: Whether the protection timer uses instantaneous voltage, fault-window minimum voltage, or another implementation detail.
Whether VIBR1_2 requires scaling/filtering before it can be used as paper Vs.

next_recommendation.required_manual_confirmation_before_impl: Boundary convention has been selected for the project as Vs <= 0.20 pu -> tVRT = 0 s -> immediate_trip; timer implementation semantics and VIBR1_2 base/sampling still need confirmation before actual PSCAD trip logic.
